title: EDR Architecture Analysis
when_to_use: Use EDR Architecture Analysis to develop professional-grade edr architecture analysis capabilities at Mastery level. This recipe is part of the Custom Implant Engineering pillar and integrates with linked missions OM-M05.

steps.title: EDR Sensor Architecture Mapping
steps.instruction: Map complete EDR architecture: user-mode sensor (process, callbacks, file monitoring), kernel driver (kernel callbacks, filter drivers), cloud backend (telemetry ingestion, alerting). Identify: trusted application whitelists, exclusion paths, evasion-susceptible components. Document communication protocol between sensor and cloud. Use Frida, Process Monitor, and network sniffing to trace sensor behavior.
steps.tip: Most EDRs have documented architectures in whitepapers. Supplement public info with dynamic analysis.
steps.expected_output: EDR architecture diagram + component interaction map

steps.title: Kernel Driver Reverse Engineering
steps.instruction: Reverse engineer EDR kernel driver: identify callback registration, filter driver chains, telemetry collection routines. Analyze driver with IDA Pro or Ghidra. Identify vulnerability classes: buffer overflows in callback handlers, race conditions in telemetry queuing, privilege escalation paths. Map driver exports and IOCTL handlers.
steps.tip: Kernel drivers are complex; focus on attack surface: IOCTLs, callbacks, and inter-process communication.
steps.expected_output: Kernel driver analysis with vulnerability assessment

steps.title: Behavioral Telemetry Analysis
steps.instruction: Analyze what behaviors trigger detection. Execute known attack techniques, observe EDR response. Catalog: process injection methods (CreateRemoteThread vs NtMapViewOfSection), file operations (in excluded vs non-excluded directories), registry writes (HKCU vs HKLM). Identify detection thresholds: how many suspicious events trigger alert?
steps.tip: Behavioral analysis requires patience and systematic testing. Build matrix: technique vs detection result.
steps.expected_output: Behavioral telemetry matrix with detection thresholds

steps.title: Cloud Telemetry & Alert Correlation
steps.instruction: Analyze cloud backend: telemetry ingestion pipeline, alert correlation rules, investigation tools. Identify: alert false-positive thresholds, alert silencing mechanisms, investigation timeline limits (how far back does backend store data?). Interview or research: how does EDR decide between log alert vs immediate response?
steps.tip: Cloud backend decisions depend on telemetry quality and rule tuning. Understand alert hierarchy.
steps.expected_output: Cloud architecture analysis + alert correlation mapping

steps.title: Adversary Emulation
steps.instruction: Execute multiple adversary personas within EDR: benign business processes, lateral movement, data exfiltration. Document: which behaviors trigger alert immediately, which generate low-confidence alerts, which evade entirely. Measure: time between behavior and alert, alert content, investigation timeline.
steps.tip: Adversary emulation tests EDR completeness. Include both noisy (command & control callback) and stealthy (credential dumping) scenarios.
steps.expected_output: Adversary emulation report with detection results

steps.title: Mitigation & Hardening Recommendations
steps.instruction: Identify EDR shortcomings and recommend improvements: enhanced behavioral analytics, stronger cloud correlation rules, better kernel driver verification, improved threat hunting capabilities. Provide constructive feedback on architecture. Suggest complementary controls (SIEM integration, network telemetry, hardware-based monitoring).
steps.tip: EDR vendors appreciate detailed technical feedback. This report demonstrates mastery of both sides.
